Which sorting algorithm is most natural for linked lists? Think about one that involves splitting and merging.
Merge sort works well because you can split a linked list in half using the slow/fast pointer technique.
After splitting, recursively sort both halves, then merge them — just like merge two sorted lists.
For O(1) space, consider a bottom-up merge sort: iteratively merge sublists of increasing size (1, 2, 4, ...).
